description: This group of plots presents mechanical properties of Fe-30Mn-1.2C-xAl (x=0, 2, 4, 6, 8 wt.%) austenitic steels under as-homogenised condition and different aging treatments (450°C/1h, 500°C/1h/24h, 550°C/1h/24h, 600°C/1h/24h) from Rapid Alloy Prototyping experiments. Subplot (a) shows yield stress (YS, unit MPa), (b) shows ultimate tensile stress (UTS, unit MPa), (c) shows total elongation (TE, unit %), (d) shows hardness (unit HBW). Key trends: as Al content increases to 8 wt.%, YS, UTS and hardness of aged samples increase drastically, e.g., 550°C 24h aged 8Al sample reaches ~940 MPa YS, ~1020 MPa UTS, ~360 HBW, while TE drops significantly (as low as ~7% for 600°C 24h aged 8Al sample). This figure demonstrates that increasing Al content and appropriate aging treatment greatly improve strength of the steels via κ-carbide precipitation at the cost of ductility, supporting microstructure-property correlation for low-density Fe-Mn-Al-C steel design.
